questions.question: Two fair dice are rolled. By counting ordered outcomes, what is P(sum = 4)?

explanations.A: Correct. 3 of the 36 equally likely ordered pairs sum to 4, so P = 3/36 = 1/12.
explanations.B: This matches a different sum's count, not the 3 ordered pairs giving 4.
explanations.C: This uses 12 as the denominator instead of 36, the true number of ordered outcomes for two dice.
explanations.D: This overcounts the ordered pairs that sum to 4; only 3 do.
